title: İstanbul Galata Üniversitesi
content:
ÜNİVERSİTEMİZ

Anasayfa

/

Üniversite

/

ÜNİVERSİTEMİZ

/

Duyurular

12 Ağustos 2024

Architect and Artist Beste Aykut will be presenting her photography exhibition, "Echoes of Japan: The Art of Urban and Natural Contrasts," with an opening reception on Friday, August 23, 2024, at 5:30 PM at Frej Coffee & Arthouse, at Istanbul Galata University's Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences.

This exhibition presents a photographic journey through Japan’s essence, as seen through the eyes of a creative visionary. This collection explores the profound dance between nature and architecture, a delicate yet striking relationship that captures the essence of Japanese culture.

Rendered in evocative black and white, these photographs offer a timeless view of Japan’s serene natural landscapes alongside the intricate tapestry of its urban scenes. Each image captures moments of stillness and motion, tranquility, and vibrancy, revealing the subtle nuances that shape Japan’s unique and compelling aesthetic, weaving a shared visual language that reflects the soul of landscapes and cityscapes.

The exhibition showcases the ethereal beauty of cherry blossoms in full bloom, the silent majesty of ancient temples, the vibrant energy of Tokyo’s streets, and the quiet grace of everyday life. The monochromatic palette accentuates the textures and architectural forms, inviting deeper contemplation of the depicted scenes.

“Echoes of Japan” transcends a mere photographic exhibition; it is an invitation to ponder the harmony and tension between natural and built environments. It celebrates contrasts and testifies to the enduring allure of Japan’s diverse landscapes. This visual odyssey encourages an appreciation of the delicate balance between nature and urbanity, revealing the profound beauty inherent in the contrasts that shape our surroundings.

The exhibition will be on display until August 30, 2024
